prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "C-Skip 跑姿练习" (C-skip running drill) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the top phase of a C-skip drill: right foot on the ball of the foot pushing off the ground; left thigh driven up to parallel with the floor (knee at hip height) but with the knee extending forward and downward in a circular C-shaped motion (like cycling pedaling), foot dorsiflexed; arms in running-style opposition; torso upright; this is the classic C-skip drill (cycling motion); plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: hip flexors and quadriceps and hamstring of the cycling leg, plus calf muscles of the supporting leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "髂腰肌 / 股四头肌" pointing to the hip flexors and quadriceps; "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstring group; "小腿 / 臀大肌" pointing to the calf and glute area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
